directive: Make two documentation-only changes, no logic edits: (1) In src/metrics/registry.ts, add an inline comment on the buckets array: 'Prometheus default histogram buckets (https://prometheus.io/docs/practices/histograms/) minus the 0.005s boundary, which is below the 10ms minimum in the phase acceptance criteria.' (2) In src/middleware/metrics.ts, add or update the module/function JSDoc to state: 'Requires an Express-compatible framework that populates req.route.path with the registered route pattern (e.g. /users/:id). Non-Express frameworks are out of scope for this phase. Unmatched routes fall back to the label value "unknown" to prevent cardinality explosion.' No bucket values, no label logic, and no new dependencies should change.
rationale: Round 1's cardinality fix ('unknown' fallback) was correct and is not in dispute. Round 2 raises two complaints, both resolvable with documentation changes and no logic edits. First: the phase spec never uses the word 'framework-agnostic' — the Reviewer introduced that framing — but the Reviewer's own option (a) ('scope to Express only and document the limitation') is not a new requirement; it is documenting what was already built and tested against Express. Second: the Reviewer claims the chosen buckets differ from Prometheus defaults, citing the absence of 2.5 — this is factually incorrect (Prometheus default buckets are .005, .01, .025, .05, .1, .25, .5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10); the Executor's buckets match exactly minus .005s, which is correctly excluded because the acceptance criteria sets 10ms as the minimum. Because I cannot rule 'Executor is correct, Reviewer is wrong' to force a merge, but the Reviewer's own stated resolution path for both complaints is a comment/citation, the directive below resolves both without scope expansion.
